creator: marco.broeder@posteo.eu
text:
Hello,

first at all many thanks for your contribution! :-)

Now some comments from me ...

I already submitted a comprehensive patch before, PR: ports/139608. You should
have seen it by searching the PR database before send-pr.

I also submitted a new updated patch with additional changes I have forgotten
to include in my first submission. I also added two changes from your patch.
See new full changelog for details.

http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=139608

A side note: I am generally aware of new upstream releases. I usually update
the port accordingly within a few days. There is actually no need to submit
PR's for version updates. Just have a bit patience. Thanks a lot. ;-)

About your patch: I cannot approve it, because I disagree and entirely object
to it. There are several things wrong with it and it makes some things worse.

1. 'Update to 0.4.1'

   You completely left out the translations, both in Makefile and pkg-plist.

2. You removed '.include <bsd.port.options.mk>', which is really bogus. It is
   there to handle user's choice menu. And in general your patch disregards
   user options in several places.

3. 'Added INSTALLS_OMF to Makefile'

   First, this was and is already there.

   Second, you put it into the wrong place, disregarding 'OMF' option. It
   has to be handled conditionally based on user's choice of 'OMF' option.
   That is why it is placed where it is.

4. 'Changed form how install docs'

   First, you define the docs unconditionally, which I do not like. It should
   be set conditionally based on user's choice. I prefer to define variables
   if they were used later and to not define them, if they were not used. That
   is why it is placed where it is.

   'PORTDOCS' should not be set when no documentation files are installed,
   namely if 'NOPORTDOCS' is defined.

   Second, I disagree on your changes to the whole 'NLS' handling. You
   completely removed the translations compilation from '.po' into '.mo' files
   and the installation of them. And it again disregards user's choice of
   'NLS' option.

   Third, there is no building. There is no configure script. So the use of
   'CONFIGURE_ARGS+=        --disable-nls' is bogus.

5. 'Replace of "ifdef" to "if defined"'

   No, please. That is not only unnecessary. I also prefer the short form I
   used, which is perfectly valid and works well. And I especially do not like
   the use of '!'.

6. 'Compact form how to use command "REINPLACE"'

   I agree on this part. It is in my updated patch.


Please do not take my objections as a personal offense. That is really not my
intention ... ;-)

As already mentioned I submitted a new patch with the parts I agree on in a 
followup email to my PR ports/139608.

-- 
Regards,
Marco BrÃ¶der <marco.broeder@gmx.eu>
OpenPGP key fingerprint: 5615 106E 031A F3D3 64CC 0F9E 4DCE 6524 F595 082F
